children.heading: Excess inclusions may not be offset by net operating losses
children.label: a Excess inclusions may not be offset by net operating losses

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The taxable income of any holder of a residual interest in a REMIC for any taxable year shall in no event be less than the excess inclusion for such taxable year.

children.heading: Special rule for affiliated groups
children.label: 2 Special rule for affiliated groups
children.text: All members of an affiliated group filing a consolidated return shall be treated as 1 taxpayer for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: in determining under section 172 the amount of any net operating loss for such taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: in determining taxable income for such taxable year for purposes of subsection (a)(2)(B)(ii)(I) and the second sentence of subsection (b)(2) of section 172.

children.label: A
children.text: the reference in section 55(b)(1)(D) to taxable income shall be treated as a reference to taxable income determined without regard to this subsection,

children.label: B
children.text: the alternative minimum taxable income of any holder of a residual interest in a REMIC for any taxable year shall in no event be less than the excess inclusion for such taxable year, and

children.label: C
children.text: any excess inclusion shall be disregarded for purposes of computing the alternative tax net operating loss deduction.

children.heading: Organizations subject to unrelated business tax
children.label: b Organizations subject to unrelated business tax
children.text: If the holder of any residual interest in a REMIC is an organization subject to the tax imposed by section 511, the excess inclusion of such holder for any taxable year shall be treated as unrelated business taxable income of such holder for purposes of section 511.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount taken into account with respect to such interest by the holder under section 860C(a), over

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the daily accruals with respect to such interest for days during such calendar quarter while held by such holder.

children.label: i
children.text: the adjusted issue price of such interest at the beginning of such quarter, and

children.label: ii
children.text: 120 percent of the long-term Federal rate (determined on the basis of compounding at the close of each calendar quarter and properly adjusted for the length of such quarter).

children.label: i
children.text: increased by the amount of daily accruals for prior quarters, and

children.label: ii
children.text: decreased (but not below zero) by any distribution made with respect to such interest before the beginning of such quarter.

children.heading: Federal long-term rate
children.label: C Federal long-term rate
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “Federal long-term rate” means the Federal long-term rate which would have applied to the residual interest under section 1274(d) (determined without regard to paragraph (2) thereof) if it were a debt instrument.

children.heading: Treatment of residual interests held by real estate investment trusts
children.label: d Treatment of residual interests held by real estate investment trusts
A
children.text: the aggregate excess inclusions determined with respect to such interests, over

children.label: B
children.text: the real estate investment trust taxable income (within the meaning of section 857(b)(2), excluding any net capital gain),
any amount allocated to a shareholder under paragraph (1) shall be treated as an excess inclusion with respect to a residual interest held by such shareholder.

children.heading: Tax on transfers of residual interests to certain organizations, etc.
children.label: e Tax on transfers of residual interests to certain organizations, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A tax is hereby imposed on any transfer of a residual interest in a REMIC to a disqualified organization.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount (determined under regulations) equal to the present value of the total anticipated excess inclusions with respect to such interest for periods after such transfer, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the highest rate of tax specified in section 11(b).

children.heading: Liability
children.label: 3 Liability
children.text: The tax imposed by paragraph (1) on any transfer shall be paid by the transferor; except that, where such transfer is through an agent for a disqualified organization, such tax shall be paid by such agent.

children.label: A
children.text: the transferee furnishes to such person an affidavit that the transferee is not a disqualified organization, and

children.label: B
children.text: as of the time of the transfer, such person does not have actual knowledge that such affidavit is false.

children.label: A
children.text: the United States, any State or political subdivision thereof, any foreign government, any international organization, or any agency or instrumentality of any of the foregoing,

children.label: B
children.text: any organization (other than a cooperative described in section 521) which is exempt from tax imposed by this chapter unless such organization is subject to the tax imposed by section 511, and

children.label: C
children.text: any organization described in section 1381(a)(2)(C).

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of excess inclusions for such taxable year allocable to the interest held by such disqualified organization, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the highest rate of tax specified in section 11(b).

children.label: i
children.text: any regulated investment company, real estate investment trust, or common trust fund,

children.label: ii
children.text: any partnership, trust, or estate, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any organization to which part I of subchapter T applies.

children.heading: Tax to be deductible
children.label: C Tax to be deductible
children.text: Any tax imposed by this paragraph with respect to any excess inclusion of any pass-thru entity for any taxable year shall, for purposes of this title (other than this subsection), be applied against (and operate to reduce) the amount included in gross income with respect to the residual interest involved.

children.heading: Exception where holder furnishes affidavit
children.label: D Exception where holder furnishes affidavit
i
children.text: the record holder of such interest furnishes to such pass-thru entity an affidavit that such record holder is not a disqualified organization, and

children.label: ii
children.text: during such period, the pass-thru entity does not have actual knowledge that such affidavit is false.

children.label: A
children.text: within a reasonable time after discovery that the transfer was subject to tax under paragraph (1), steps are taken so that the interest is no longer held by the disqualified organization, and

children.label: B
children.text: there is paid to the Secretary such amounts as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Administrative provisions
children.label: 8 Administrative provisions
children.text: For purposes of subtitle F, the taxes imposed by this subsection shall be treated as excise taxes with respect to which the deficiency procedures of such subtitle apply.

children.heading: Treatment of variable insurance contracts
children.label: f Treatment of variable insurance contracts
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, with respect to any variable contract (as defined in section 817), there shall be no adjustment in the reserve to the extent of any excess inclusion.
